rendering_strategy.rationale: This marketplace platform has three distinct content tiers requiring different rendering strategies. (1) Static editorial content — onsen guides, etiquette articles, regional spotlights, and the landing page — should use SSG for maximum performance and SEO, as these pages change infrequently and benefit from CDN edge caching. (2) Ryokan listing pages should use ISR with 60-second revalidation — property details change infrequently but availability and pricing may update daily, and ISR provides SEO benefits of static generation with near-real-time data freshness. (3) Search results, booking flow, user dashboard, host management dashboard, and community feed require SSR for personalized, real-time content that depends on user session, search parameters, and live availability. Next.js App Router natively supports all three strategies per-route, making this hybrid approach natural without additional infrastructure.

alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: Unnecessary server cost for static content (guides, landing pages). Listing pages would incur repeated DB queries on every request when ISR can serve cached pages. Increases Time to First Byte (TTFB) for pages that don't need real-time data.

Impossible for personalized search results and booking flows. With potentially thousands of listing pages, full static generation at build time would create long build times. Availability data would be stale immediately after build.

Poor SEO for listing and editorial pages — critical for organic discovery by both Japanese and international travelers. Initial load would be slow, and content would not be indexable by search engines without additional SSR layer.

packages.name: next
packages.purpose: Core framework — App Router for hybrid rendering (SSR/SSG/ISR), file-based routing, server components, API routes for booking endpoints

packages.name: next-intl
packages.purpose: Internationalization framework for Japanese/English bilingual support — handles locale routing, message formatting, date/number localization, and server component i18n

packages.name: @supabase/supabase-js
packages.purpose: Supabase client for Postgres database access — listings, bookings, user profiles, reviews. Provides real-time subscriptions for availability updates and Row Level Security for multi-tenant host data

packages.name: next-auth
packages.purpose: Authentication — email/password for Japanese users, Google/Apple OAuth for international users, host vs. guest role management

packages.name: stripe
packages.purpose: Server-side Stripe SDK for payment processing — booking charges, host payouts via Stripe Connect, refund handling for cancellations, support for Japanese card networks (JCB)

packages.name: zod
packages.purpose: Schema validation for booking forms, listing creation, search parameters, and API request/response validation — shared between client and server

packages.name: zustand
packages.purpose: Lightweight client state management for search filters, booking flow state, photo gallery state, and UI preferences (language, currency)

packages.name: date-fns
packages.purpose: Date manipulation for availability calendar logic, check-in/check-out date handling, cancellation deadline calculations, and Japanese date formatting (令和 era support)

packages.name: react-day-picker
packages.purpose: Accessible date range picker component for booking date selection — supports disabled dates (blocked availability), minimum stay enforcement, and Japanese locale

packages.name: embla-carousel-react
packages.purpose: Touch-friendly image carousel for listing photo galleries — lightweight, performant, supports swipe gestures on mobile for photo-forward ryokan browsing

packages.name: react-map-gl
packages.purpose: Mapbox GL wrapper for React — onsen region map exploration, ryokan location pins, interactive area-based search with clustering for dense onsen towns

packages.name: swr
packages.purpose: Client-side data fetching with caching and revalidation — used for availability checks, search-as-you-type results, and real-time booking status updates

external_apis.name: Supabase (hosted Postgres + Auth + Storage)
external_apis.usage: Primary data store for listings, bookings, reviews, user profiles. Supabase Storage for ryokan photos (with image transformations).
external_apis.rate_limits: Free tier: 500MB DB, 1GB storage, 50K auth users. Pro tier ($25/mo): 8GB DB, 100GB storage, unlimited auth. No API rate limits on Pro tier — connection pooling via pgBouncer handles concurrency.
external_apis.cost_implications: Pro tier at $25/month sufficient for MVP. At scale (500+ listings, 5K bookings/month), expect $100–200/month. Storage costs scale with photo uploads — estimate 50 photos/listing × 500 listings = 25,000 photos at ~2MB each = ~50GB.
external_apis.fallback: Can migrate to self-hosted Postgres on Railway or Neon if Supabase pricing becomes unfavorable. Supabase client is a thin wrapper over PostgREST — migration path is clear.

external_apis.name: Stripe (Payments + Connect)
external_apis.usage: Guest payment collection, host payout via Stripe Connect Express accounts, cancellation refund processing, webhook-driven booking status updates.
external_apis.rate_limits: 100 read requests/second, 100 write requests/second in live mode. No hard limit on payment volume.
external_apis.cost_implications: 3.6% + ¥40 per transaction for Japanese cards. Connect platform fee is additional 0.25% + ¥10 per payout. At ¥30,000 average booking value, Stripe fees are ~¥1,120/booking. Our 8% commission (¥2,400) minus Stripe fees leaves ~¥1,280 net margin per booking.
external_apis.fallback: Komoju as alternative for Japanese-specific payment methods (convenience store payment, bank transfer) if Stripe Japan coverage is insufficient.

external_apis.name: Mapbox GL JS
external_apis.usage: Interactive map for onsen region exploration, ryokan location display, area-based search with bounding box queries.
external_apis.rate_limits: 50,000 free map loads/month, then $5 per 1,000 loads. Geocoding: 100,000 free requests/month.
external_apis.cost_implications: Free tier likely sufficient for first 6 months. At 100K monthly map views, cost ~$250/month. Static map images for listing cards can reduce interactive map loads.
external_apis.fallback: MapLibre GL JS (open-source fork) with OpenStreetMap tiles as zero-cost alternative. Requires self-hosting tile server but eliminates vendor lock-in.

external_apis.name: Vercel Image Optimization
external_apis.usage: On-the-fly image resizing, format conversion (WebP/AVIF), and CDN delivery for ryokan photos via next/image component.
external_apis.rate_limits: Pro plan: 5,000 source images optimized/month included, then $5 per 1,000.
external_apis.cost_implications: With 25,000 listing photos and responsive variants, may exceed free tier quickly. Consider Supabase Storage image transformations as primary, with Vercel as CDN layer only.
external_apis.fallback: Cloudinary free tier (25,000 transformations/month) or Supabase Storage built-in transformations.

serverless_function_timeout.limit: Pro plan: 60 seconds (default 10s). Hobby plan: 10 seconds.
serverless_function_timeout.impact: Booking creation involves Stripe payment intent + DB write + confirmation email — must complete within 10s on Hobby or 60s on Pro. Stripe API calls typically resolve in 1–3 seconds, so this is feasible.
serverless_function_timeout.mitigation: Use Vercel Pro plan ($20/month) for 60s timeout. For long-running tasks (bulk photo upload, report generation), use Vercel background functions or offload to Supabase Edge Functions.

bundle_size.limit: 250MB compressed for serverless functions. 50MB per Edge Function.
bundle_size.impact: Mapbox GL JS is the largest client dependency (~200KB gzipped). Server-side Stripe SDK is lightweight (~50KB). Total client bundle should stay well under limit with code splitting.
bundle_size.mitigation: Dynamic import Mapbox only on map-view pages. Use Next.js built-in code splitting per route. Lazy-load image carousel component.

edge_function_limitations.limit: Edge Functions run on V8 isolates — no Node.js-specific APIs (fs, child_process). 128MB memory limit.
edge_function_limitations.impact: Middleware for locale detection and auth checks can run on Edge for low latency. Stripe webhooks and DB queries must use standard serverless functions (Node.js runtime).
edge_function_limitations.mitigation: Use Edge Runtime only for middleware (locale routing, auth redirect). All API routes use Node.js runtime.

cold_start.limit: Serverless cold starts: 200–500ms typical for Node.js. Edge: <50ms.
cold_start.impact: Search and listing pages may experience cold start latency. For a booking platform, 500ms cold start is acceptable but not ideal for search-as-you-type.
cold_start.mitigation: Use SWR for client-side search with optimistic UI. Keep frequently-hit API routes warm via cron pings. Use Edge middleware for instant locale detection.

deployment_regions.limit: Serverless functions deploy to a single region by default. Edge functions deploy globally.
deployment_regions.impact: Primary user base is Japan (domestic + international tourists in Japan). Latency to Tokyo region is critical.
deployment_regions.mitigation: Set Vercel function region to hnd1 (Tokyo). Use Edge middleware for global locale routing. CDN serves static assets globally for international users browsing from home.

browser_apis.usage: Optional 'nearby onsen' feature — detect user's location to suggest closest onsen regions. Useful for travelers already in Japan.
browser_apis.fallback: Default to IP-based approximate geolocation (via Vercel's geo headers: x-vercel-ip-city, x-vercel-ip-country). Prompt user to manually select region if geolocation is denied.

browser_apis.usage: Lazy loading of listing card images as user scrolls through search results. Essential for photo-heavy listing grids.
browser_apis.fallback: next/image handles lazy loading natively with loading='lazy' attribute. Intersection Observer provides additional control for infinite scroll pagination. Supported in all modern browsers (>97% global coverage).

browser_apis.usage: Native share sheet for sharing ryokan listings on mobile — 'Share this ryokan' button triggers OS-level share dialog (LINE, Twitter, WhatsApp).
browser_apis.fallback: Fall back to copy-to-clipboard with toast notification on unsupported browsers (desktop Safari, Firefox). Show manual share buttons for major platforms.

browser_apis.usage: Auto-detect user's preferred language (Japanese vs. English) for initial locale selection.
browser_apis.fallback: Vercel Edge middleware reads Accept-Language header server-side before page render. Client-side navigator.language used for confirmation. Manual language switcher always available in header.

browser_apis.usage: Responsive image gallery layout — dynamically adjust photo grid columns based on container width for ryokan listing pages.
browser_apis.fallback: CSS Container Queries or Tailwind responsive breakpoints handle most cases. ResizeObserver used only for complex masonry-style photo layouts. Supported in all modern browsers.
